LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WILLIAM C. LYNN, TRUSTEE OF THE WILLIAM C. LYNN TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 13, 2014, ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.
ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WILLIAM C. LYNN AND WILLIAM C. LYNN, TRUSTEE OF THE WILLIAM C. LYNN TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT- THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION TO FORECLOSE ON THE MECHANIC'S LIEN AS UNOPPOSED. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSES OF ACTION. A VIOLATION OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SEC. 17918 IS A CURABLE DEFECT AND THE PROPOSED 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT NOW ALLEGES COMPLIANCE. A FACTUAL ISSUE REMAINS REGARDING WHETHER REGISTERING CALISCO RATHER THAN CALISCO BUILDERS & PAINTING WAS PROPER AND ALLOWS PLAINTIFFS TO PROCEED. THE PROPOSED 2ND AC ALSO ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF CASTRO WAS DULY LICENSED DURING ALL RELEVANT TIMES. WHETHER THAT ALLEGATION IS IN FACT TRUE RAISES A FACTUAL ISSUE THAT CANNOT BE RESOLVED ON DEMURRER. BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SEC. 7159 IS NOT A BASIS TO BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. THE PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE CONTRACTS WERE IN WRITING. IN ADDITION, COURTS MAY ENFORCE ORAL HOME IMPROVEMENT CONTRACTS IN CERTAIN INSTANCES. SEE HINERFELD-WARD V. LIPIAN (2010) 188 CAL.APP.4TH 86. LASTLY, PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMPLIFY THE AGENCY ALLEGATIONS AND PLEAD ANY OTHER APPLICABLE CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-25-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-14-2014 Continued To Sep-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David B. Golden, Esq. of Golden Kopcke, Tel. 415.399.9994, for defendant and moving party;
Ellen Rodin, Esq. of the Law Office of Ellen Rodin, Tel. 914.714.3714, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WILLIAM C. LYNN, TRUSTEE OF THE WILLIAM C. LYNN TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is stricken in its entirety pursuant to CCP 436(b). The Court sustained Defendant's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint with leave to amend on February 13, 2014. Plaintiffs failed to amend the complaint within 10 days as required by CRC 3.1320(g). When Defendant moved to have the First Amended Complaint dismissed for failure to timely amend in April 2014, the Court issued an Order requiring Plaintiffs to file and serve the SAC by April 25, 2014. Plaintiffs failed to comply with this Order and did not serve the SAC until May 21, 2014. Plaintiffs fail to make a showing of good cause for why the Court's Order was not complied with. The evidence submitted in support of Plaintiffs' opposition is not properly authenticated and may not be considered. Even if the Court were to consider it, the evidence shows that Plaintiffs' counsel's car troubles did not begin until April 26, 2014, after the deadline to file and serve the SAC. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-13-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID B. GOLDEN OF GOLDEN & KOPCKE, TEL. NO. 399-9994 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WILLIAM C. LYNN AND WILLIAM C. LYNN, TRUSTEE OF THE WILLIAM C. LYNN TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT- The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to the 8th cause of action to foreclose on the mechanic's lien as unopposed. The Court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend as to the breach of contract causes of action. A violation of Business & Professions Code sec. 17918 is a curable defect and the proposed 2nd Amended Complaint now alleges compliance. A factual issue remains regarding whether registering Calisco rather than Calisco Builders & Painting was proper and allows plaintiffs to proceed. The proposed 2nd AC also alleges that plaintiff Castro was duly licensed during all relevant times. Whether that allegation is in fact true raises a factual issue that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Business & Professions Code sec. 7159 is not a basis to bar plaintiff's claims. The proposed amended complaint alleges that the contracts were in writing. In addition, courts may enforce oral home improvement contracts in certain instances. See Hinerfeld-Ward v. Lipian (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 86. Lastly, plaintiff has leave to amplify the agency allegations and plead any other applicable claim. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
